examples.en: I've been feeling down lately.
examples.vi: Dạo này tôi cảm thấy buồn.

examples.en: She felt down after hearing the news.
examples.vi: Cô ấy buồn sau khi nghe tin.

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: You look sad. Are you feeling down?

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: Yeah, I'm feeling a bit down today.

examples.en: Cheer up! It's not the end of the world.
examples.vi: Vui lên! Không phải tận thế đâu.

examples.en: I tried to cheer her up with a joke.
examples.vi: Tôi cố gắng làm cô ấy vui bằng một câu chuyện cười.

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: Why are you so sad?

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: I failed my exam.

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: Oh, cheer up! You can try again.

examples.en: She's in a good mood today.
examples.vi: Hôm nay cô ấy có tâm trạng tốt.

examples.en: I'm in a good mood because the sun is shining.
examples.vi: Tôi có tâm trạng tốt vì nắng đẹp.

collocations: put someone in a good mood

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: Why is he so cheerful?

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: He's in a good mood because he got a promotion.

examples.en: He tends to lose his temper easily.
examples.vi: Anh ấy thường dễ mất bình tĩnh.

examples.en: I almost lost my temper with the rude customer.
examples.vi: Tôi suýt mất bình tĩnh với vị khách thô lỗ.

collocations: try not to lose your temper

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: Did you see how angry he got?

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: Yes, he really lost his temper.

examples.en: Take it easy and relax.
examples.vi: Thư giãn và nghỉ ngơi đi.

examples.en: You should take it easy after work.
examples.vi: Bạn nên thư giãn sau giờ làm.

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: I'm so stressed about the exam.

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: Take it easy. You've studied hard.

examples.en: I feel like going for a walk.
examples.vi: Tôi cảm thấy muốn đi dạo.

examples.en: Do you feel like eating out tonight?
examples.vi: Bạn có muốn ăn ngoài tối nay không?

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: What do you want to do?

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: I feel like watching a movie.

examples.en: Have you made up your mind yet?
examples.vi: Bạn đã quyết định chưa?

examples.en: I can't make up my mind about the job offer.
examples.vi: Tôi không thể quyết định về lời mời làm việc.

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: Which dress should I buy?

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: Make up your mind soon; the sale ends today.

examples.en: She changed her mind at the last minute.
examples.vi: Cô ấy thay đổi ý định vào phút cuối.

examples.en: You can always change your mind if you want.
examples.vi: Bạn luôn có thể thay đổi ý định nếu muốn.

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: I thought you were going to the party.

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: I changed my mind. I'm too tired.

examples.en: His constant complaining gets on my nerves.
examples.vi: Việc than vãn liên tục của anh ấy làm tôi khó chịu.

examples.en: The noise is getting on my nerves.
examples.vi: Tiếng ồn đang làm tôi khó chịu.

collocations: start to get on my nerves

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: Why are you so annoyed?

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: That loud music is getting on my nerves.

examples.en: I'm fed up with this weather.
examples.vi: Tôi chán ngấy thời tiết này rồi.

examples.en: She's fed up with his excuses.
examples.vi: Cô ấy chán ngấy những lời bào chữa của anh ta.

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: You look upset.

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: I'm fed up with my job. I need a change.

examples.en: In my opinion, this is the best solution.
examples.vi: Theo ý kiến của tôi, đây là giải pháp tốt nhất.

examples.en: In my opinion, she should accept the offer.
examples.vi: Theo ý kiến của tôi, cô ấy nên chấp nhận lời đề nghị.

collocations: in my opinion, it's better to

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: What do you think about the plan?

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: In my opinion, it's too risky.

examples.en: As far as I'm concerned, the matter is closed.
examples.vi: Theo như tôi quan tâm, vấn đề đã kết thúc.

examples.en: As far as I'm concerned, you can do what you want.
examples.vi: Theo như tôi quan tâm, bạn có thể làm gì bạn muốn.

collocations: as far as I'm concerned, it's fine
as far as I'm concerned, no
as far as I'm concerned, yes

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: Do you mind if I leave early?

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: As far as I'm concerned, it's okay.

examples.en: We have different opinions, so let's agree to disagree.
examples.vi: Chúng ta có ý kiến khác nhau, vậy hãy đồng ý bất đồng.

examples.en: Sometimes it's best to agree to disagree.
examples.vi: Đôi khi tốt nhất là đồng ý bất đồng.

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: I think the movie was great.

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: I didn't like it at all.

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: Let's agree to disagree.

meaning_vi: có sự đắn đo, do dự

examples.en: I'm having second thoughts about the trip.
examples.vi: Tôi đang đắn đo về chuyến đi.

examples.en: She had second thoughts after signing the contract.
examples.vi: Cô ấy do dự sau khi ký hợp đồng.

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: Are you still going to quit your job?

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: I'm having second thoughts now.

examples.en: We are of the same mind on this issue.
examples.vi: Chúng tôi cùng quan điểm về vấn đề này.

examples.en: Are you of the same mind as me?
examples.vi: Bạn có cùng quan điểm với tôi không?

collocations: be of the same mind about
be of the same mind as someone
be of the same mind on that

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: I think we should invest more in education.

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: I'm of the same mind.

examples.en: She feels strongly about animal rights.
examples.vi: Cô ấy cảm thấy mạnh mẽ về quyền động vật.

examples.en: I don't feel strongly about that issue.
examples.vi: Tôi không có cảm xúc mạnh mẽ về vấn đề đó.

example_dialogue.speaker: A
example_dialogue.text: What do you think about the new policy?

example_dialogue.speaker: B
example_dialogue.text: I feel strongly that it's unfair.
